ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Anatolius, Deacon at Constantinople.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Thy Love has written to me that our most pious lord orders a successor to be appointed to my most reverend brother John, bishop of Prima Justiniana, on account of the ailment of the head from which he suffers, lest perchance that city, while without the jurisdiction of a bishop, should be ruined by its enemies, which God forbid.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And yet the canons nowhere enjoin that a bishop should be superseded on account of sickness.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And it is altogether unjust that, if bodily ailments come on, the sick person should be deprived of his dignity.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Accordingly this thing can by no means be done through us, lest sin should come upon my soul from his deposition.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But it is to be suggested that, if he who bears rule is sick, an administrator may be found, to undertake all his charge, and maintain and fill his place, without his being deposed, in the government of the Church and custody of the city; so that neither may Almighty God be offended nor the city be found to be neglected.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If, however, the same most reverend John should haply on account of his ailments request to be relieved from the dignity of the episcopate, it should be conceded on his presenting a petition in writing.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But otherwise we are altogether unable, with due regard to the fear of Almighty God, to do this thing.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if he should be unwilling thus to make petition, what pleases the most pious Emperor, whatever he commands to be done, is in his power.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: As he determines, so let him provide.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Only let him not cause us to be mixed up in the deposition of one so situated.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Still, what he does, if it is canonical, we will follow.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if it is not canonical, we will bear it, so far as we can without sin of our own.
